## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On September 29, 1938, the absolutely infamous, incredibly massive, and historically catastrophic Munich Agreement was officially and aggressively signed. Leaders of Great Britain, France, and Italy heavily capitulated to the fierce demands of Adolf Hitler, aggressively allowing Nazi Germany to absolutely annex the highly strategic Sudetenland region of Czechoslovakia. This massive, failed diplomatic policy of appeasement fiercely emboldened Hitler, absolutely guaranteeing that the massive, devastating carnage of World War II would erupt less than a year later.
